conditions: Coronary artery disease (history of prior cardiac event)
medications: Nitroglycerin 0.4 mg sublingual as needed for chest pain
Omeprazole 20 mg daily as needed for reflux
Acetaminophen as needed for joint pain (avoids NSAIDs due to kidney disease)
health.mental_health: Since his youngest moved out last year, Ryan has had low mood and a loss-of-purpose feeling, with occasional anxiety about aging and health. He keeps it private at work and mostly copes by staying busy, but sleep has been inconsistent.
recent_visits: Cardiology follow-up at NYU Langone (med review and blood pressure check)
Nephrology consult at Mount Sinai (early CKD monitoring, diet guidance, labs ordered)
Primary care annual physical (A1c and lipid panel, vaccine updates discussed)
Nutrition counseling session for low-sodium, kidney-friendly eating

finance.income_range: $62,000–$78,000/year (base plus occasional overtime and shift differentials)
finance.debt: Carries about $11k on a credit card from helping his daughter with a security deposit and furnishing her first apartment; also paying down a small personal loan (~$6k) used for an emergency home repair. He worries about medical copays and keeping up with NYC rent on a single income.
investments: I bonds (small holding from a few years ago)
recent_transactions: Paid $320 in specialist copays and lab fees (cardiology/nephrology)
Sent $750 to daughter via Zelle for moving-related expenses
Paid $189 for Con Edison bill (winter spike)
Charged $96 for a blood pressure monitor and pill organizer from a local pharmacy
Withdrew $200 cash for union dues and incidental expenses

relationships: Marisol Soto (wife, lives with him; relationship strained by the 'quiet house' and different coping styles)
Elena Soto (adult daughter in Queens; he checks in often and provides occasional financial help)
Miguel Soto (adult son in Jersey City; less frequent contact, feels Ryan is 'too focused on work')
Luis Rivera (older cousin in the Bronx; his main confidant for family and health worries)
A small circle of coworkers he sometimes meets for coffee after late shifts, but he avoids talking about his health
social.social_media_activity: Mostly passive on Facebook (likes family photos, follows NYC news and Yankees updates). Rarely posts; recently shared a couple of old family vacation photos and a 'throwback' post that got supportive comments, which he found comforting but also embarrassing.
private_conversations: Admitted to his cousin that he feels 'replaceable' now that the kids are gone and worries he has nothing outside work
Discussed with his wife a fear of another heart event and whether he should retire earlier than planned
Talked with his daughter about how much money he can realistically send without hurting his own bills
Confided to a coworker that he sometimes drinks more than he should on lonely nights, then feels guilty

schedule.work_hours: Full-time, rotating shifts; typically 3:00pm–11:30pm four days/week and 7:00am–3:30pm one day/week, with occasional weekend coverage and overtime during conventions/holidays.
regular_appointments: Blood pressure check at a local pharmacy kiosk every Sunday morning
Monthly cardiology check-in or telehealth message review (as needed based on symptoms/labs)
Nephrology labs every 3 months (creatinine/eGFR, urine albumin)
Weekly short walk group in Central Park (weather permitting)
upcoming_events: Nephrology lab draw and follow-up appointment next month
Hotel staff training session on new property-management system (PMS) next week
Dinner in Queens for his daughter's birthday (planned for a Monday he requested off)
Union meeting about staffing changes and overtime rules
schedule.daily_routine: Up late due to evening shifts; coffee and a light breakfast, short walk or errands midday, then work. After work he decompresses with TV and sometimes a late snack; tries to take evening meds consistently and logs blood pressure when he remembers.

professional.company: A mid-size unionized business hotel near Times Square (part of a national chain franchise)
projects: Training front desk agents on the new PMS rollout and updated ID/credit-card verification procedures
Reducing guest compensation costs by tightening service-recovery guidelines
Cross-training night audit coverage to address staffing gaps
Updating VIP arrival notes and coordinating with housekeeping for faster room turns
professional.performance_issues: Over the past few months he has had a couple of missed handoff details and one tense guest escalation. He suspects it’s tied to poor sleep and feeling emotionally 'flat' since the kids left, and he worries management is watching his reliability.
workplace_relationships: Generally respected by long-tenured agents, but a newer assistant manager questions his slower adoption of the new system
Strong working relationship with the head concierge, who often helps de-escalate difficult guests
Occasional friction with housekeeping leadership over late checkouts and room readiness

hobbies: Long walks along the Hudson River Greenway
Watching Yankees games and old boxing matches
Cooking simple Puerto Rican comfort foods (now trying lower-sodium versions)
Photography with a phone camera (street scenes, architecture)
Volunteering once a month at a neighborhood food pantry when his schedule allows
habits: Tracks blood pressure sporadically in a notebook and on his phone
Limits salt most days but slips during late-night takeout after shifts
Has 1–2 drinks some nights to unwind; more on particularly lonely weekends (a habit he hides from his kids)
Avoids NSAIDs due to kidney disease and double-checks OTC meds
Keeps the TV on at night for background noise because the apartment feels too quiet
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom apartment in Washington Heights with his wife; the kids’ rooms are now mostly storage, and he hasn’t known what to do with the extra space since they left.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Trying to follow a heart-healthy, kidney-conscious diet: lower sodium, moderate protein, more vegetables, fewer processed foods; still relies on deli sandwiches or bodega meals when working doubles.
